content.problemStatement:
Chef loves cats, and tries to search for them everywhere.

Today, Chef saw a string SSS of length 333, consisting of lowercase English letters only.

Your task is to tell Chef whether he can rearrange the letters of the string SSS to form the word "cat".


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains a single string SSS of length 333, containing only lowercase English letters.


Output Format
Output the answer on a single line: YES\text{YES}YES if some letters of SSS can be used to form the word "cat", and NO\text{NO}NO otherwise.


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e. the strings NO,No,nO,\text{NO}, \text{No}, \text{nO},NO,No,nO, and no\text{no}no will all be treated as equivalent.
constraints: SSS is a string of length 333.
SSS contains only lowercase English letters, i.e. the characters 'a' through 'z'.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
No rearrangement is needed: the word is already "cat".

Explanation:
It's not possible to rearrange "tic" to form "cat".

Explanation:
"tac" can be rearranged to form "cat".
